title: Digging into the past
content_html:
Last year, Kate Marshall was given a very exciting opportunity – to help her father, who’s a history lecturer, at a historical site! He was leading a team to dig up and explore the area.

When they arrived, though, the site wasn’t quite what Kate had expected.

[16] So the first job was to remove it all and prepare the land for digging. Says Kate, ‘Dad hadn’t warned me I’d have to work so hard as soon as we got there!’

But when the site was completely cleared, the team found pieces of ancient pots on the ground. However, those weren’t as exciting as everyone had thought. Kate’s dad told them that the important pots were still under the ground. And because no-one had touched them for centuries, the team would learn far more about their history – but first they would have to dig deeper. Says Kate, [17] ‘But we all knew Dad was right!’

The site was divided into small squares, with a leader for each square, who told everyone how to dig. ‘That wasn’t as easy as it sounded, either,’ Kate reports. ‘Instead of just digging great big holes, we all had to dig really carefully, and remove small amounts of soil each time. [18] So it made sense.’

‘My friends at home were really interested in what I was doing,’ Kate explains. ‘They kept texting me to ask what I’d found. [19] But actually, we were looking for ordinary, everyday objects that could tell us about the people who’d lived in the area centuries ago.’

In the end, though, Kate wasn’t disappointed by what she found. ‘One day, when I was digging away, I found a stone with a strange shape. [20] Someone had obviously made it hundreds of years ago, which meant it was really important. So Dad cleaned it up, and said it would go to the nearby museum. So I was pleased that at last, I’d found something interesting!’

options.text: It turned out to be a small figure of a horse.
No-one made that mistake, luckily.
That way, everyone made sure they didn’t miss anything.
In fact, the whole area was actually still covered in grass.
It was a bit sad to see it disappear.
Some people were a bit disappointed by that news.
They probably imagined it was things like gold jewellery.
It was a new experience for me, too.

explanations.html:
16. D: [Reference / Context]
Đáp án D đúng vì câu sau có cụm "remove it all", nên "it" phải chỉ về thứ vừa được nhắc đến trước đó. Câu D nói cả khu vực vẫn còn covered in grass, nên hoàn toàn hợp logic: việc đầu tiên là phải dọn sạch lớp cỏ đó rồi mới chuẩn bị mặt đất để đào. Ngoài ra, câu trước nói nơi khảo cổ không giống Kate tưởng tượng, và việc thấy cả khu vực còn đầy cỏ chính là chi tiết giải thích điều đó.

17. F: [Reaction / Contrast]
Đáp án F đúng vì ngay trước chỗ trống, bố Kate nói rằng những thứ quan trọng vẫn còn ở dưới đất và nhóm sẽ phải đào sâu hơn nữa. Tin này khiến một số người hơi thất vọng, nên câu F nối rất tự nhiên. Câu sau bắt đầu bằng "But we all knew Dad was right!", tạo thế tương phản rõ ràng: tuy hơi thất vọng, mọi người vẫn biết bố Kate nói đúng.

18. C: [Purpose / Result]
Đáp án C đúng vì đoạn trước mô tả cách đào rất cẩn thận: không đào hố lớn mà chỉ lấy từng ít đất một. Câu "That way, everyone made sure they didn’t miss anything" giải thích mục đích của cách làm đó: nhờ đào chậm và kỹ, họ không bỏ sót hiện vật nào. Vì vậy câu sau "So it made sense" hoàn toàn hợp mạch.

19. G: [Expectation contrast]
Đáp án G đúng vì trước chỗ trống là việc bạn bè Kate nhắn tin hỏi cô đã tìm được gì, còn sau chỗ trống có "But actually", báo hiệu một sự đối lập giữa điều người ta tưởng tượng và thực tế. Câu G cho thấy bạn bè cô có lẽ nghĩ nhóm khảo cổ đang tìm những món quý như gold jewellery. Nhưng thực ra họ tìm ordinary, everyday objects để hiểu cuộc sống con người trong quá khứ.

20. A: [Identification / Explanation]
Đáp án A đúng vì trước chỗ trống Kate nói cô tìm thấy một hòn đá có hình dạng lạ. Câu A giải thích ngay hòn đá đó thực ra là gì: "a small figure of a horse". Sau đó câu "Someone had obviously made it hundreds of years ago" tiếp tục làm rõ rằng đây là một vật do con người tạo ra từ rất lâu trước, nên có giá trị lịch sử.

title: The story of the carrot and the ring
content_html:
Have you ever lost something that was precious to you, and thought you’d never see it again? That’s what happened to Mary Grams from Canada.

Mary had worn her engagement ring for more than half a century when she suddenly lost it. She was working in her garden on the family farm one day, pulling out a plant. [16] When she discovered it was missing, she searched everywhere for it for days, before finally giving up.

Mary realised she probably wasn’t going to find the ring again. So she decided not to tell anyone about what had happened, apart from her son. Instead, she thought she would replace it with a much cheaper ring from a jeweller’s. [17] In fact, it was so similar that no-one else in her family even knew her original ring was missing.

But the story has a happy ending. Mary eventually got her engagement ring back – 13 years after it was lost! One day Mary’s daughter-in-law Colleen was working in the same garden where Mary had been all those years ago.

[18] But this time it looked a bit different – because there was a carrot growing right through the middle of it!

As soon as Colleen saw the ring, she knew immediately who the owner was. The farm had been in the family for over 100 years. [19] So when Colleen discovered the story of the lost ring, it became clear there was only one person it could possibly have belonged to, and the ring was returned to Mary.

Mary decided to remove her ring from the carrot and wear it again. [20] Then the ring was washed, and Mary put it back on her finger – and it still fitted perfectly!

options.text: She was digging up vegetables there when she discovered the ring.
That gave her a very good idea about what to do with the carrot.
And that was probably when the ring came off her finger.
So she carefully cut the carrot in half.
She had never seen anything like it before.
And only two women had lived there in all that time.
It was tiring work, as some of them were very big.
Luckily, she managed to find another one that looked just like it.

explanations.html:
16. C: [Time reference / Sequence]
Đáp án C đúng vì câu trước nói Mary đang pulling out a plant, còn câu sau nói When she discovered it was missing. Câu And that was probably when the ring came off her finger nối hai ý này rất chặt: chiếc nhẫn có lẽ rơi ra đúng lúc bà đang nhổ cây. Từ that quay lại chỉ khoảnh khắc vừa được nhắc tới ở câu trước.

17. H: [Reference / Result]
Đáp án H đúng vì trước chỗ trống Mary định replace chiếc nhẫn đã mất bằng một chiếc rẻ hơn từ tiệm kim hoàn. Câu Luckily, she managed to find another one that looked just like it cho biết bà đã tìm được một chiếc khác giống chiếc nhẫn gốc. Câu sau In fact, it was so similar... tiếp tục khẳng định chiếc nhẫn thay thế giống đến mức mọi người trong gia đình không nhận ra.

18. A: [Event sequence]
Đáp án A đúng vì câu trước giới thiệu Colleen đang làm việc trong cùng khu vườn đó, còn câu sau mở đầu bằng But this time it looked a bit different. Trước khi nói chiếc nhẫn trông khác đi, đoạn văn cần nêu rõ Colleen đã tìm thấy nó như thế nào. Câu She was digging up vegetables there when she discovered the ring phù hợp hoàn toàn.

19. F: [Explanation / Deduction]
Đáp án F đúng vì câu trước nói trang trại đã thuộc về gia đình hơn 100 năm. Câu And only two women had lived there in all that time bổ sung một dữ kiện quan trọng giúp suy luận. Vì chỉ có hai phụ nữ từng sống ở đó, nên khi Colleen biết câu chuyện về chiếc nhẫn bị mất, cô dễ dàng kết luận người chủ chỉ có thể là Mary.

20. D: [Action sequence]
Đáp án D đúng vì câu trước nói Mary muốn remove her ring from the carrot, nên bước tiếp theo hợp lý nhất là cắt củ cà rốt ra để lấy chiếc nhẫn. Câu So she carefully cut the carrot in half mô tả đúng hành động đó. Sau đó câu Then the ring was washed nói tiếp bước kế tiếp trong chuỗi hành động.

title: Computer game exhibition
content_html:
Have you ever tried playing the kind of video games that your parents played? The Museum of Science in Manchester, in the UK, has held an exhibition for the last few years, which invites visitors to do exactly that. It offers them the chance to play games from the last 40 years, in various sessions throughout the day.

These video games sessions have now become one of the main attractions of the museum. They are full of people every day, playing a wide range of games.

[16] For parents, for example, these are usually the games they used to play in their childhood.

There’s also an educational purpose to the games. For instance, some old types of computer, dating back 40 years, are also available in the sessions. They were originally used in classrooms to teach pupils to write their own computer programs.

[17] Now, the museum is holding workshops that encourage children to learn similar skills – and they’re still very popular.

The sessions are also seen as social events, as people discover how much fun it is to play video games with other family members. And there’s also an area at Power Up! where a number of visitors can sit down together. [18] And nowadays, this is often how fans of video games are more likely to experience playing.

The exhibition also shows how much progress technology has made over the last 40 years. Parents can often remember playing very simple games. But the games that are played today are more complex. [19] And the players also have to use much more complicated techniques.

However, one serious side of the exhibition is that organisers also want to show that video gaming is an important industry, employing many skilled people.

[20] That way, people who enjoy gaming will also understand all the hard work, talent and imagination that goes into creating these amazing games.

options.text: Visitors each pay for 90-minute sessions.
And at the time, it helped lots of young people to do that.
So they hope the exhibition will share this message.
But not everyone thinks it’s a lot of fun.
However, visitors often choose the ones they’re familiar with.
They have better storylines and animation, too.
They also create the music to go with the game.
Then they can all enjoy playing the same game.

explanations.html:
16. E: [Contrast / Reference]
Đáp án E đúng vì câu trước nói visitors có thể chơi a wide range of games, còn câu sau nói For parents, for example, these are usually the games they used to play in their childhood. Câu However, visitors often choose the ones they’re familiar with tạo liên kết rất chặt: dù có nhiều trò chơi, khách tham quan thường chọn những trò quen thuộc với mình. Từ these ở câu sau quay lại chỉ the ones they’re familiar with.

17. B: [Reference / Past to present link]
Đáp án B đúng vì ngay trước chỗ trống, đoạn văn nói những máy tính cũ từng được dùng trong lớp học để teach pupils to write their own computer programs. Cụm do that trong đáp án B quay lại đúng hành động write their own computer programs. Sau đó câu Now, the museum is holding workshops... chuyển mạch từ quá khứ sang hiện tại rất tự nhiên.

18. H: [Result / Social activity]
Đáp án H đúng vì câu trước nói có một khu vực nơi several visitors can sit down together. Kết quả hợp lý tiếp theo là Then they can all enjoy playing the same game. Câu sau And nowadays, this is often how fans of video games are more likely to experience playing tiếp tục phát triển ý chơi game theo nhóm, mang tính xã hội.

19. F: [Explanation / Comparison]
Đáp án F đúng vì đoạn này đang so sánh old games và modern games. Câu trước nói game ngày nay are more complex, nên câu They have better storylines and animation, too là phần giải thích rất phù hợp về việc chúng phức tạp hơn ở điểm nào. Sau đó câu And the players also have to use much more complicated techniques bổ sung thêm hệ quả đối với người chơi.

20. C: [Purpose / Result]
Đáp án C đúng vì câu trước nói organisers muốn cho thấy video gaming là một ngành công nghiệp quan trọng, sử dụng nhiều lao động có kỹ năng. Câu So they hope the exhibition will share this message nối trực tiếp với ý đó. Sau đó That way giải thích mục đích: bằng cách truyền tải thông điệp này, người yêu game sẽ hiểu hơn công sức, tài năng và trí tưởng tượng cần có để tạo ra game.

title: The giant piano
content_html:
A young man in New Zealand recently became famous for making one of the largest pianos ever.

Adrian Mann, who now works as a professional piano builder, started building the piano when he was just 16 years old, and made many of the parts for it himself. The piano is 5.7 metres long and is very heavy. And the keyboard actually has keys that are a metre in length. [16] But despite this difference in size, the instrument can still be played perfectly, thanks to Adrian’s hard work.

[17] In fact, the whole thing started when Adrian became interested in the materials used for the wires inside the piano. These create the notes when they’re hit.

And he found that if the wires were really long, he could get an amazing sound. From there, he went on to build the whole piano.

The instrument was kept inside a church in his town for some time, before Adrian decided it was time to transfer the huge piano from the church to his workshop. [18] So in the end, the fire service had to come and help take the instrument to its new home.

Since the move, a lot of piano players have visited Adrian to try out the piano. But Adrian says that when they arrive, some people aren’t very positive about the piano.

[19] But actually, it always performs brilliantly. They soon discover that the piano can play a wide range of music, just like any normal piano.

There’s been so much interest in Adrian’s piano that he could probably start making and selling others just like it. [20] So, at the moment, he has no plans to make any more.

options.text: However, he put a lot of work into his original model.
It’s so big, you could imagine actually lying down inside it!
So what gave Adrian the idea to create such a huge piano?
They expect that the instrument won’t sound very good.
But things weren’t always that simple.
That’s much larger than on a normal-sized piano.
Most of them were surprised by the wonderful result.
But he soon found he couldn’t move it out on his own.

explanations.html:
16. F: [Comparison / Reference]
Đáp án F đúng vì câu trước nói các phím đàn dài tới một mét. Câu That’s much larger than on a normal-sized piano bổ sung phép so sánh trực tiếp với đàn piano bình thường. Sau đó câu But despite this difference in size cho thấy this difference in size chính là sự khác biệt vừa được nêu ở câu F.

17. C: [Question and explanation]
Đáp án C đúng vì câu sau mở đầu bằng In fact và giải thích nguồn gốc của toàn bộ ý tưởng. Vì vậy trước đó hợp lý nhất là một câu hỏi dẫn vào: So what gave Adrian the idea to create such a huge piano? Câu sau trả lời trực tiếp cho câu hỏi này.

18. H: [Cause and effect]
Đáp án H đúng vì đoạn này nói Adrian muốn chuyển cây đàn khổng lồ từ nhà thờ về xưởng. Câu But he soon found he couldn’t move it out on his own giải thích vì sao ngay sau đó fire service phải đến giúp. Mối liên kết logic là: không tự chuyển được → phải gọi người hỗ trợ.

19. D: [Expectation vs reality]
Đáp án D đúng vì câu trước nói some people aren’t very positive about the piano. Câu They expect that the instrument won’t sound very good làm rõ sự nghi ngờ của họ. Sau đó But actually, it always performs brilliantly tạo sự đối lập rõ ràng giữa điều họ nghĩ ban đầu và thực tế.

20. A: [Contrast / Reason]
Đáp án A đúng vì câu trước nói Adrian có thể làm và bán thêm nhiều cây đàn tương tự do có rất nhiều người quan tâm. Tuy nhiên câu sau lại nói hiện tại anh chưa có kế hoạch làm thêm. Câu However, he put a lot of work into his original model giải thích hợp lý cho quyết định đó: mẫu đầu tiên đã tốn quá nhiều công sức.

title: A new way of making electricity
content_html:
Ever since the 19th century, when people were developing different ways of creating electricity, companies have looked for improved ways of producing power, using cleaner and more efficient methods.

Now a UK company called Pavegen has been working on technology that could be widely used in the future to produce electricity. [16] The company has developed a special type of floor, made of square tiles. Underneath each square, there’s a system that can produce a certain amount of electricity very cheaply – from the energy created every time someone steps on it!

The creator of the floor, Laurence Kemball-Cook, came up with the idea when he was a student, and did some work experience with an energy company. The company asked him to investigate ways of providing street lighting in city centres, using energy from the sun to produce electricity.

[17] One reason was that many city centres don’t get enough sunlight, because of all the tall buildings. Then he thought of a better plan.

[18] The right technology could be used to convert this energy into electricity – right under people’s feet!

The design of the floor is actually extremely effective. [19] And the reason is that a lot of energy is produced simply because of the large numbers of people walking across the floor. In fact, the special squares are already in place in several locations with high numbers of pedestrians. These include big department stores and also an airport.

The flooring can have other uses too, such as recording how many people visit a shopping centre at particular times. [20] For example, shop owners in the centre would be interested in knowing at which times of days they have the highest customer numbers. So the next time you visit a big shopping centre, have a careful look at the floor that you’re walking across!

options.text: But that isn’t the end of the story.
This kind of information is very useful for certain people.
Why not use the energy created by pedestrians instead?
However, one big problem could be cost.
And it’s actually based on a simple idea.
This is especially true when it’s been used in very busy areas.
But it soon became clear that this might not work.
This new system should work even better.

explanations.html:
16. E: [Introduction / Explanation]
Đáp án E đúng vì câu trước giới thiệu công nghệ mới của Pavegen, còn câu sau bắt đầu giải thích cấu tạo cụ thể của loại sàn. Câu And it’s actually based on a simple idea nối rất tự nhiên: trước hết giới thiệu công nghệ, sau đó nói rằng ý tưởng của nó thực ra khá đơn giản, rồi mới giải thích cơ chế tạo điện từ bước chân.

17. G: [Problem / Cause]
Đáp án G đúng vì ngay trước chỗ trống, Laurence đang xem xét cách dùng năng lượng mặt trời để cung cấp điện cho đèn đường. Câu sau giải thích một lý do khiến cách đó không hiệu quả là nhiều trung tâm thành phố không có đủ ánh sáng mặt trời do các tòa nhà cao. Vì vậy, But it soon became clear that this might not work là câu nối hợp logic nhất.

18. C: [Idea development / Reference]
Đáp án C đúng vì câu trước nói Laurence nghĩ ra một kế hoạch tốt hơn, còn câu sau nói The right technology could be used to convert this energy into electricity. Cụm this energy phải quay lại một nguồn năng lượng vừa được nêu ra, và chỉ có energy created by pedestrians là phù hợp. Vì vậy câu C khớp hoàn toàn.

19. F: [Result / Detail]
Đáp án F đúng vì câu trước nhận xét rằng thiết kế của sàn cực kỳ hiệu quả. Câu sau giải thích lý do: nhiều năng lượng được tạo ra nhờ số lượng lớn người đi bộ bước qua nó. Câu This is especially true when it’s been used in very busy areas làm rõ rằng hiệu quả này đặc biệt cao ở những nơi rất đông người.

20. B: [Use / Example]
Đáp án B đúng vì câu trước nói loại sàn này còn có thể ghi lại số người đến trung tâm thương mại vào các thời điểm nhất định. Câu sau đưa ví dụ về shop owners muốn biết khi nào có đông khách nhất. Vì vậy, This kind of information is very useful for certain people là câu chuyển ý hợp lý nhất từ chức năng của sàn sang lợi ích thực tế của dữ liệu đó.

title: What’s the point in studying music?
content_html:
Many children have music classes when they attend school. And it’s thought that music can really help children with learning other subjects.

For example, one research project looked at what happened when a class of children were divided into groups and given a simple task to do, with one group listening to music while completing it, and the other completing the task in silence. [16] The first group performed better than the second. So this seems to suggest that music can improve performance in certain areas.

So how exactly can you benefit from studying music? According to some studies, musical training can develop the part of your brain that’s involved with language, so you can understand your own language better. [17] And that’s a very useful skill to have. What’s more, young people who’ve studied music also seem to score more highly in other areas such as maths. [18] For example, reading music includes learning about quarter and half notes, which are basically fractions, like in maths. And when you’re learning about rhythm, you’re counting the notes in a piece of music. So they do appear to be connected.

Music also lets you explore new ideas, think in a creative way, and gain in confidence. If you’re learning the guitar, for example, it can be really exciting when you’re able to start inventing your own pieces of music. And when you do that, you’re practising your listening skills because you have to listen carefully to the music you’re making. [19] It’s certainly essential when you join an orchestra, for example.

One of the biggest benefits, of course, is that listening to music helps you to be less stressed. [20] That should always be in a relaxed atmosphere, though, to be effective. And who knows? Maybe your musical knowledge will open up a great career path for you in the future!

options.text: Students have also shared their own ideas about music.
And creating music can make you feel the same way.
So it could be that these school subjects are linked in some way.
These explain why music affects us in certain ways.
And there was a difference between the two.
It could also help with learning a second one.
But it isn’t really what’s happening.
This is particularly important when performing with other people.

explanations.html:
16. E: [Comparison / Result]
Đáp án E đúng vì trước chỗ trống bài đọc nhắc đến hai nhóm học sinh: một nhóm nghe nhạc khi làm bài, nhóm còn lại làm trong im lặng. Câu And there was a difference between the two là câu nối hợp lý nhất, vì the two quay lại chỉ hai nhóm này. Câu sau The first group performed better than the second tiếp tục giải thích rõ sự khác biệt đó là gì.

17. F: [Reference / Skill development]
Đáp án F đúng vì câu trước nói học nhạc giúp bạn hiểu ngôn ngữ của chính mình tốt hơn. Câu It could also help with learning a second one tiếp tục phát triển đúng chủ đề ngôn ngữ. Từ one ở đây thay cho language, còn câu sau And that’s a very useful skill to have quay lại đúng ý học thêm một ngôn ngữ thứ hai.

18. C: [Linking ideas / Explanation]
Đáp án C đúng vì trước chỗ trống bài nói học sinh học nhạc thường đạt kết quả tốt hơn ở maths. Câu So it could be that these school subjects are linked in some way là câu kết luận chuyển tiếp rất phù hợp. Sau đó các câu ví dụ giải thích mối liên hệ giữa music và maths qua fractions, counting notes và rhythm.

19. H: [Reference / Group performance]
Đáp án H đúng vì câu trước nói về listening skills khi tạo nhạc. Câu This is particularly important when performing with other people dùng This để quay lại listening skills. Câu sau It’s certainly essential when you join an orchestra, for example là ví dụ cụ thể cho việc biểu diễn cùng người khác, nên H khớp hoàn toàn.

20. B: [Emotional effect / Reference]
Đáp án B đúng vì câu trước nói listening to music helps you to be less stressed. Câu And creating music can make you feel the same way tiếp tục phát triển ý lợi ích cảm xúc của âm nhạc. Cụm the same way quay lại trạng thái less stressed, relaxed. Câu sau That should always be in a relaxed atmosphere, though, to be effective nối tiếp tự nhiên với hoạt động âm nhạc vừa được nhắc đến.

title: Bees play games too!
content_html:
We know that some animals love playing games. Dogs, for example, spend hours running after sticks. But recently, scientists have carried out experiments using bees and small wooden balls. [16] And the idea that the insects do so is making experts ask questions about insect intelligence.

In one experiment, bees were put in a box with separate spaces, rather like rooms. The bees could move around the box into the different ‘rooms’. One contained pollen, bees’ favourite food. The bees had to get from their starting point at one side of the box to the area with the food. They could choose one of two routes through spaces containing small wooden balls. One area had balls which were stuck to the floor. [17] The scientists discovered that as the experiment was repeated, the bees chose the route through this second room much more frequently, stopping to ‘play’ with the balls as they passed. Some bees were seen moving the balls over 100 times. This again made scientists believe that the insects were doing it just for fun. [18] Therefore, there had to be a particular reason for the bees’ decision.

In another experiment, two rooms were used, one painted blue and the other yellow. The scientists put the small wooden balls in just one of these rooms. In this experiment, the balls were not stuck to the floor. The bees were then allowed in to explore the areas. [19] When the bees were then put back in again, scientists could clearly see a difference in which room they entered. For example, if the balls had been in the yellow room, more bees chose that room, even though the balls were no longer there.

Scientists also found that results varied depending on individual bees, with younger ones pushing the balls more often than older ones. [20] Adult males spent longer on the activity than females, for example.

options.text: Despite this, the scientists knew their ideas were correct.
In the other, however, they could be moved.
That wasn’t the only difference seen.
The method therefore produced an unusual result.
They say the results show bees also have the ability to enjoy an activity.
After a while, the bees and the balls were removed.
Such changes were challenging for smaller bees.
After all, both ways led to a reward.

explanations.html:
16. E: [Reference / Meaning]
Đáp án E đúng vì câu sau có cụm do so, nên nó phải quay lại một ý vừa được nêu ở câu trước. Câu They say the results show bees also have the ability to enjoy an activity cung cấp chính xác nội dung đó: ong cũng có khả năng tận hưởng một hoạt động. Vì vậy do so = enjoy an activity.

17. B: [Contrast / Reference]
Đáp án B đúng vì câu trước nói One area had balls which were stuck to the floor, nên chỗ trống phải mô tả khu vực còn lại để tạo tương phản. Câu In the other, however, they could be moved khớp hoàn toàn. Từ this second room ở câu sau cũng xác nhận bài đang nói tới căn phòng thứ hai với các quả bóng có thể di chuyển.

18. H: [Reasoning / Logic]
Đáp án H đúng vì đoạn trước nói ong thường dừng lại chơi với bóng ở một tuyến đường. Tuy nhiên lựa chọn đó không thể do thức ăn, vì cả hai con đường đều dẫn tới phần thưởng. Câu After all, both ways led to a reward giải thích chính xác vì sao câu sau kết luận there had to be a particular reason for the bees’ decision.

19. F: [Sequence]
Đáp án F đúng vì câu trước nói ong được cho vào để khám phá các khu vực, còn câu sau nói When the bees were then put back in again. Nếu ong được cho vào lại, điều đó có nghĩa là trước đó chúng đã bị lấy ra. Câu After a while, the bees and the balls were removed nối mạch thời gian rất tự nhiên.

20. C: [Addition / Comparison]
Đáp án C đúng vì câu trước đã nêu một sự khác biệt: ong trẻ đẩy bóng thường xuyên hơn ong già. Câu That wasn’t the only difference seen báo hiệu còn một khác biệt khác nữa, và câu sau đưa ví dụ cụ thể: ong đực trưởng thành dành nhiều thời gian cho hoạt động này hơn ong cái.

title: An incredible journey
content_html:
A small boat built by high school students from the American state of New Hampshire has made an incredible journey. The boat, around 1.5 metres in length, is called Rye Riptides. The adventure started when a teacher at Rye Junior High School ordered a kit for a project to make a boat. The kit was supplied by a company called Educational Passages, which aims to use boat-building projects to develop children’s interest in science and technology. [16] For example, because the kit includes technology which sends information about the boat’s position back to a computer, they learned about how waves move around the oceans.

The students spent two years making the boat, and also chose items to send with it on its journey. [17] The hope was that anyone finding the boat later would be interested in seeing things like these from another country. After students had some help from Educational Passages, the boat was ready to start its journey. The students were very excited. Some believed it would reach European shores. [18] At first, they were able to follow Rye Riptides as it moved across the Atlantic Ocean and marked the route on a map.

Then, in September 2021, Rye Riptides stopped sending a signal. The students knew that there were huge waves in the Atlantic Ocean. [19] But this wasn’t what happened. Only a few months later, another signal was received. Rye Riptides had landed on Smøla Island off the coast of Norway. Educational Passages helped again, using social media to contact a local school.

A boy called Karel Nuncic saw her message. Karel went to Smøla Island and found the boat. [20] Luckily, though, the objects the students had put inside were safe, and Karel took the boat back to show his classmates. The two groups of students have since connected online, making friends and learning about new cultures.

options.text: It was covered in small sea creatures and badly damaged.
The result was exactly what the students wanted.
These included photos and coins that were placed inside.
Others, however, were not so sure.
It hasn’t been easy to decide what they should do.
At the same time, it helps increase their understanding of the oceans.
Despite this, the boat had travelled a huge distance.
They thought that these had destroyed the boat.

explanations.html:
16. F: [Addition / Example link]
Đáp án F đúng vì câu trước nói dự án đóng thuyền giúp phát triển sự hứng thú của trẻ với khoa học và công nghệ, còn câu sau mở đầu bằng For example để đưa ví dụ cụ thể. Câu At the same time, it helps increase their understanding of the oceans bổ sung thêm một lợi ích nữa, rồi câu sau giải thích cụ thể việc học sinh hiểu hơn về đại dương nhờ công nghệ theo dõi vị trí con thuyền.

17. C: [Reference]
Đáp án C đúng vì câu trước nhắc đến items to send with the boat, còn câu sau có cụm things like these. Từ these phải quay lại chỉ những món đồ vừa được nêu, và câu These included photos and coins that were placed inside cung cấp đúng thông tin đó.

18. D: [Contrast]
Đáp án D đúng vì ngay trước chỗ trống là Some believed it would reach European shores. Câu Others, however, were not so sure tạo thế đối lập trực tiếp giữa some và others. Đây là mạch ý rất tự nhiên: một số học sinh tin con thuyền sẽ tới châu Âu, nhưng những người khác thì không chắc.

19. H: [Reference / Cause and effect]
Đáp án H đúng vì câu trước nói there were huge waves in the Atlantic Ocean. Trong đáp án H, these quay lại chỉ huge waves, còn they chỉ the students. Câu sau But this wasn’t what happened phủ nhận suy đoán đó, tức là những con sóng lớn không thực sự phá hủy con thuyền.

20. A: [Description / Contrast]
Đáp án A đúng vì câu trước nói Karel tìm thấy con thuyền, còn câu sau mở đầu bằng Luckily, though, cho thấy trước đó phải có một chi tiết tiêu cực để tạo tương phản. Câu It was covered in small sea creatures and badly damaged phù hợp hoàn toàn: dù con thuyền bị hư hại nặng, may mắn là những đồ vật bên trong vẫn an toàn.

title: Andrew Edston: Young Chef
content_html:
Andrew Edston is a 20-year-old chef with a great future.

He recently won a competition called The Future Chef, for professional chefs in the part of the UK where he works. He’s already taken part in various cooking contests but says this latest was the toughest. Even before it started, he knew he’d be competing against talented chefs. [16] As he explains, he always enters competitions convinced he’s going to win!

Although competitions can be enjoyable, cooking contests are not always just for fun. Restaurant owners often enter their young chefs into competitions as part of their training. This is what happened to Andrew. In the kitchen at Garden Tastes, the restaurant where he works, he is usually in charge of one course. [17] Doing so has allowed him to gain important experience.

The people judging the food are usually top food industry experts, which is extremely useful. Andrew gets to present his food to important people and hear their comments on every dish. [18] In fact, in the restaurant, it’s more likely that customers wanting to speak to the chef will be complaining about a problem! And even when customers take the time to say they like something, it’s not the same as hearing this from a food expert.

Andrew’s already planning to enter a national cooking competition called Top Chef. If he gets to the final, he’ll cook live on TV. It’s a huge challenge, so he’s getting advice from the other chefs at work. [19] For one thing, it’ll be great advertising for the restaurant! They also think he deserves success, knowing how much he puts into getting everything right. For example, one of the dishes Andrew’s planning contains cheese. Rather than using something he’d cooked with before, he visited many farms and tasted hundreds of different cheeses. [20] It’s attention to details like this which he hopes will impress judges!

options.text: They all want to see him do well.
The ingredients are given to the chefs.
In contests, though, he plans a whole menu.
If the dish is popular, however, he cooks it again.
Having the opportunity to get opinions like this is rare.
He kept going until he’d found one with the perfect flavour.
That didn’t stop him aiming high though.
He is expecting to be quite nervous.

explanations.html:
16. G: [Contrast / Attitude]
Đáp án G đúng vì câu trước nói Andrew biết mình sẽ phải cạnh tranh với những đầu bếp rất tài năng. Câu That didn’t stop him aiming high though cho thấy dù biết đối thủ mạnh, anh vẫn đặt mục tiêu rất cao. Câu sau tiếp tục khẳng định điều này: anh luôn bước vào cuộc thi với niềm tin mình sẽ thắng.

17. C: [Contrast / Reference]
Đáp án C đúng vì câu trước nói ở nhà hàng Andrew thường chỉ phụ trách one course. Câu In contests, though, he plans a whole menu tạo sự đối lập rõ ràng giữa công việc thường ngày và thi đấu. Cụm Doing so ở câu sau quay lại đúng hành động planning a whole menu.

18. E: [Summary / Explanation]
Đáp án E đúng vì trước chỗ trống bài nói Andrew được nghe nhận xét từ các chuyên gia ẩm thực hàng đầu. Câu Having the opportunity to get opinions like this is rare tổng kết rất hợp lý ý đó. Sau đó đoạn văn giải thích vì sao cơ hội này hiếm: trong nhà hàng, khách thường chỉ gọi đầu bếp ra khi có vấn đề.

19. A: [Reference / Support]
Đáp án A đúng vì câu trước nói Andrew đang nhận lời khuyên từ các đầu bếp khác ở chỗ làm. Câu They all want to see him do well giải thích thái độ của những người đó. Câu sau tiếp tục nêu lý do họ ủng hộ anh: quảng bá tốt cho nhà hàng và họ tin anh xứng đáng thành công.

20. F: [Result / Detail]
Đáp án F đúng vì câu trước nói Andrew đã đi nhiều trang trại và thử hàng trăm loại phô mai khác nhau. Câu He kept going until he’d found one with the perfect flavour cho thấy kết quả của quá trình tỉ mỉ đó. Câu sau It’s attention to details like this... liên kết trực tiếp với sự cẩn thận trong việc chọn đúng loại phô mai.

title: Playing board games
content_html:
If you think about games people enjoy playing, you’d probably say video games, or sports like volleyball. But many people love playing board games. There are different reasons for this. For a start, board games can usually be enjoyed by anyone who happens to be home at the time. [16] That’s because although people might have a favourite one, there is usually something for various generations to enjoy in almost any board game!

You might think that video games being so hugely popular would make board games less so; they might seem boring compared to the options available on computer. [17] Although this may seem rather surprising, the number of people playing video games has helped make game-playing in general a greater part of our lives. This has encouraged people to explore different games for their entertainment.

There are many reasons to play board games, including certain health benefits. For example, playing board games reduces the time people spend looking at screens. [18] It can also cause particular issues such as reducing how well people can concentrate. And by not looking at laptops or phones, families spend more quality time together, some even organising regular ‘games nights’.

Studies show playing games can also have the result of making events like parties or work training activities easier for shy people. They can find it simpler to get to know each other when they are involved in a game. [19] As everyone is concentrating on the same thing, rather than trying to make conversation, it is less stressful.

Games can help with confidence in other ways too. There are many decisions to be made during a game. [20] The longer we play, the more likely we are to develop a good plan for winning. With every successful turn, we see our plan is working and our confidence grows.

So what are you waiting for? Go and play a board game!

options.text: We know doing so affects the ability to sleep.
Some people, therefore, are naturally better than others.
Actually, the opposite is true.
Also, they are often suitable for different ages to play together.
Board games are easy to take to different places too.
The better someone is at making them, the more successful they will be.
Because board games have rules, such issues are avoided.
It prevents people from running out of things to say in these situations.

explanations.html:
16. D: [Addition / Explanation]
Đáp án D đúng vì câu trước nói board games có thể được bất kỳ ai ở nhà cùng chơi, còn câu sau giải thích thêm về various generations. Câu Also, they are often suitable for different ages to play together bổ sung rất hợp mạch: board games thường phù hợp để nhiều lứa tuổi cùng chơi.

17. C: [Contrast]
Đáp án C đúng vì câu trước nêu suy nghĩ rằng video games quá phổ biến sẽ làm board games kém hấp dẫn hơn. Nhưng câu sau lại bắt đầu giải thích điều ngược lại: video games đã khiến việc chơi game nói chung phổ biến hơn. Vì vậy Actually, the opposite is true là câu nối chính xác nhất.

18. A: [Cause and effect]
Đáp án A đúng vì câu trước nói chơi board games giúp giảm thời gian nhìn vào màn hình. Câu We know doing so affects the ability to sleep nêu một tác hại của việc nhìn màn hình, rồi câu sau bổ sung thêm một tác hại khác là giảm khả năng tập trung. Doing so ở đây quay lại chỉ spending time looking at screens.

19. H: [Explanation]
Đáp án H đúng vì đoạn này nói board games giúp người nhút nhát cảm thấy dễ chịu hơn trong các hoạt động xã hội. Câu It prevents people from running out of things to say in these situations giải thích rất hợp lý vì trò chơi cho mọi người một chủ đề chung để tập trung vào. Câu sau tiếp tục nói chính vì thế nên mọi việc bớt căng thẳng hơn.

20. F: [Cause and effect / Comparison]
Đáp án F đúng vì câu trước nói có nhiều quyết định phải đưa ra trong khi chơi. Câu The better someone is at making them, the more successful they will be phát triển đúng ý này: ai đưa ra quyết định tốt hơn thì thành công hơn. Sau đó đoạn văn nói thêm rằng chơi càng lâu, ta càng biết phát triển kế hoạch tốt để chiến thắng.

title: Zara Rutherford: young pilot
content_html:
Zara Rutherford is currently the youngest female pilot to fly around the world alone. In 2022, when she was 19, Zara completed her 52,000-kilometre flight in a microlight, a type of plane that is small but very fast. [16] It was one that took her through five continents and 31 countries. Before her amazing achievement, the record was held by Shaesta Waiz. Shaesta, an Afghan-born American, completed her flight at the age of 30.

Zara’s parents are both pilots and took her up in planes from an early age. It wasn’t long before she began learning to fly and eventually started wondering about doing a round-the-world trip. She knew it wouldn’t be easy. [17] It also costs a lot and can be dangerous. When Zara was finishing school, however, she decided the time was right to attempt the journey.

After completing her preparations, she started from Belgium, flying west over countries including the UK and Russia. She then flew to southeast Asia, India and the Middle East, before returning to Belgium. One of the biggest issues was that on a round-the-world flight, conditions change depending on location. [18] The mild climate in Belgium hadn’t provided opportunities to practise in different weather. The freezing temperatures of Alaska and Russia and the sandstorms in the Middle East were huge challenges. [19] In addition, she sometimes had to get her plane repaired, or collect documents to give her permission to cross certain countries.

Nevertheless, she completed her journey and felt extremely proud of herself. She is keen for other young women see her achievement, as there are not enough girls and women looking to go into subjects like engineering and technology. There are also very few female pilots. [20] After all, when people see what she has done, they might be inspired to follow her!

options.text: Her achievement could help change this in future.
This made it perfect for the huge journey she went on.
It can therefore change quickly between hot and cold.
However, she had done most of her training in Belgium.
They helped her prepare well for the trip.
These conditions meant she often had to land and wait for longer than planned.
In addition, there are other reasons for this too.
For one thing, organising everything is very complicated.

explanations.html:
16. B: [Reference / Explanation]
Đáp án B đúng vì câu trước mô tả chiếc microlight là small but very fast. Câu This made it perfect for the huge journey she went on dùng This để quay lại chính đặc điểm đó, còn it là chiếc máy bay. Câu sau tiếp tục nói về chiếc máy bay ấy đã đưa Zara qua năm châu lục và 31 quốc gia.

17. H: [Reason / Development]
Đáp án H đúng vì câu trước nói She knew it wouldn’t be easy, còn câu sau thêm một khó khăn khác là It also costs a lot and can be dangerous. Vì vậy chỗ trống cần một lý do cụ thể đầu tiên giải thích vì sao chuyến đi không dễ. Câu For one thing, organising everything is very complicated khớp hoàn toàn.

18. C: [Cause and effect]
Đáp án C đúng vì câu trước nói conditions change depending on location. Câu It can therefore change quickly between hot and cold phát triển trực tiếp ý đó, rồi câu sau đưa ví dụ cụ thể về khí hậu ôn hòa ở Bỉ, cái lạnh ở Alaska và Nga, và bão cát ở Trung Đông.

19. F: [Reference / Result]
Đáp án F đúng vì ngay trước chỗ trống là các điều kiện thời tiết khắc nghiệt như freezing temperatures và sandstorms. Câu These conditions meant she often had to land and wait for longer than planned dùng These conditions để quay lại những khó khăn đó và nêu hậu quả hợp lý. Câu sau còn bổ sung thêm những khó khăn khác về sửa máy bay và giấy tờ.

20. A: [Reference / Conclusion]
Đáp án A đúng vì câu trước nói có quá ít girls and women trong các lĩnh vực như engineering, technology và nghề phi công. Câu Her achievement could help change this in future dùng this để chỉ tình trạng thiếu nữ giới đó. Câu sau giải thích lý do: khi mọi người thấy điều Zara đã làm được, họ có thể được truyền cảm hứng để noi theo cô.

title: An incredible holiday
content_html:
For our last holiday, mum and I went to South Africa on safari – a tour where you go in a vehicle to see different animals. We started the experience by staying the night in a wooden house, where we met our safari guide, Mandisa. The house was by a lake and I was worried there might be fighting among the animals coming to drink. [16] Mandisa explained that getting water was usually the most important thing for the animals when they were there. She also explained that although they looked relaxed, each one was always looking out for danger.

The next morning, we set off in a big car. There were six seats in the back, and the sides were open, so there were no windows. It made us feel closer to the animals. [17] Mandisa was great at suggesting what would make good ones! She also taught us all about all the animals in the area.

Early in our drive, Mandisa pointed to a springbok. Springboks look like deer. She explained they are very common and are South Africa’s national animal. [18] Later, however, we saw some elephants under trees. After a while, the largest one started staring at us. Mandisa explained that there are certain types of behaviour to watch out for. [19] She told us that because the elephant wasn’t displaying any of the signs of this, we weren’t in danger. Having Mandisa there explaining everything was really helpful.

We didn’t just learn about different animals. There were special plants growing in the area too. Mandisa got us to try the leaves of one, to explain how they tasted of salt. [20] Mandisa told us that many animals get some of the salt they require from eating plants. All too soon, we had to leave. It was an experience I’ll never forget!

options.text: Knowing which one helped us get the best view.
Therefore, we realised we had to be patient.
However, there wasn’t and everything seemed very peaceful.
This is something animals need to stay healthy.
Despite this, we didn’t see any more that day!
These can show when an animal is feeling stressed.
As a result, animals quickly learn to avoid it.
It was also good because it made it easier to take incredible pictures.

explanations.html:
16. C: [Contrast / Context]
Đáp án C đúng vì câu trước nói người kể lo sẽ có fighting among the animals, còn câu sau lại giải thích về việc các con vật chủ yếu chỉ cần nước và vẫn cảnh giác nguy hiểm. Câu However, there wasn’t and everything seemed very peaceful tạo sự đối lập rất tự nhiên giữa nỗi lo ban đầu và thực tế yên bình.

17. H: [Reference]
Đáp án H đúng vì câu sau có cụm good ones, và ones ở đây phải quay lại chỉ pictures. Câu It was also good because it made it easier to take incredible pictures cung cấp đúng từ tham chiếu đó. Ngoài ra, nó cũng nối hợp lý với ý chiếc xe mở hai bên giúp mọi người gần động vật hơn.

18. E: [Contrast]
Đáp án E đúng vì câu trước nói springboks are very common, nên người đọc có thể nghĩ sẽ còn thấy nhiều con nữa. Câu Despite this, we didn’t see any more that day! tạo một sự tương phản hợp lý với ý đó. Sau đó đoạn văn chuyển sang việc sau này họ thấy voi.

19. F: [Reference / Explanation]
Đáp án F đúng vì câu trước nhắc đến certain types of behaviour to watch out for. Câu These can show when an animal is feeling stressed dùng These để quay lại chính những types of behaviour đó. Câu sau tiếp tục rất khớp khi nói con voi wasn’t displaying any of the signs of this, trong đó this = feeling stressed.

20. D: [Reference / Explanation]
Đáp án D đúng vì câu trước nói lá của một loại cây có vị muối, còn câu sau giải thích nhiều con vật lấy lượng muối cần thiết từ thực vật. Câu This is something animals need to stay healthy nối hai ý này rất tự nhiên, trong đó This quay lại chỉ salt.

title: Jamie Marshall – teenage magician!
content_html:
Doing magic tricks is not something that only older people enjoy. I’ve just been in a competition for teenage magicians – and lots of people my age took part. But the best thing was – I won! I did a trick involving taking a ring from someone in the audience. [16] Both the audience and the judges were really impressed by that unusual way of ending the trick.

My interest in magic started with a child’s magic set that I got for my birthday a few years ago. I began practising a lot, doing tricks whenever I had a spare moment. [17] But that never happened, and I went on to develop lots of new tricks.

My grandad loves magic, too. He was always doing things like producing coins from behind my ear – his way of giving me pocket money! [18] So he never showed me any of his secrets and I just had to find out for myself!

I also loved performing tricks for friends at school. And as I got better, I started performing at events like family weddings and parties. At first, I thought I’d be really nervous, but in fact, I’ve always been completely relaxed when I’m performing. [19]

What made the biggest difference to my routine, however, was taking up drama classes when I was 15. That really helped a lot. It would be great if I could work in the world of magic when I get older – and even become a famous magician! I’m not really sure what my future will hold, though. I might go into acting instead. [20] That would be cool! But whatever I end up doing, I know I’m going to really enjoy it!

options.text: And I don’t think I’d be so confident now without their help.
My parents probably thought I’d get bored with it after a while.
Actually, I’d love to be able to do both.
I still needed to do some more work on that, though.
It’s surprising really, as I used to be quite shy!
The next time they saw it, it was inside a melon that I’d cut open.
It made me start thinking about all sorts of possibilities.
But of course, people who do magic tricks never tell you how they’re done.

explanations.html:
16. F: [Reference / Result]
Đáp án F đúng vì câu trước nói Jamie lấy chiếc nhẫn từ một người trong khán giả, còn câu sau nói khán giả và giám khảo rất ấn tượng với cách kết thúc unusual đó. Câu The next time they saw it, it was inside a melon that I’d cut open mô tả chính xác màn kết thúc bất ngờ của trò ảo thuật. Từ it ở đây quay lại chỉ the ring.

17. B: [Cause and contrast]
Đáp án B đúng vì câu sau nói But that never happened, nên that phải quay lại một điều vừa được nêu ở chỗ trống. Câu My parents probably thought I’d get bored with it after a while khớp hoàn toàn: bố mẹ nghĩ Jamie sẽ chán, nhưng điều đó đã không xảy ra.

18. H: [Explanation]
Đáp án H đúng vì câu sau mở đầu bằng So he never showed me any of his secrets. Trước đó cần có một lý do giải thích vì sao ông Jamie không tiết lộ bí mật. Câu But of course, people who do magic tricks never tell you how they’re done nêu đúng nguyên tắc này.

19. E: [Comment / Contrast]
Đáp án E đúng vì câu trước nói Jamie luôn completely relaxed khi biểu diễn. Câu It’s surprising really, as I used to be quite shy! giải thích vì sao điều đó đáng ngạc nhiên: trước đây Jamie từng khá nhút nhát. Đây là một sự đối lập rất hợp lý.

20. C: [Linking ideas]
Đáp án C đúng vì câu trước nói Jamie có thể đi theo acting instead, còn trước đó lại nói về việc làm trong thế giới magic. Câu Actually, I’d love to be able to do both nối hai ý này lại với nhau. Câu sau That would be cool! cũng khớp hoàn toàn với ý làm được cả hai.

title: The lost camera
content_html:
Have you ever gone to the coast for a lovely family day out – only to discover when you return home that you’ve lost something valuable and it’s probably still on the beach?

That’s exactly what happened to a young British boy called William during a beach trip. He put his camera on top of a rock so that he could make a video of himself and his sister playing. [16] So William and his family went home, with the camera still on the rock. Later, the sea flowed onto the beach and carried it away – about 800 kilometres away, in fact, to an island off the coast of Germany.

The camera was found on the island by the couple who live there. It’s a protected area for birds, and no one else is allowed on the island without permission. [17] However, things that the sea has brought in are regularly found there, like fishing equipment and even shoes. But it was still a surprise to find a camera!

Luckily, the camera was waterproof, so the seawater hadn’t damaged it. When the couple uploaded its contents onto their computer, they were amazed to see a video of William’s beach trip. The last few seconds, however, showed only the water around the camera. [18]

The couple put one of the videos onto social media to see if anyone recognised it. [19] Then, amazingly, a special computer programme used for finding boats at sea recognised the exact place in the north of England where William had recorded the video!

A friend of William’s read about the video in a newspaper and told the family. [20] William and his father were invited to the island where the camera was discovered to see for themselves where it had finally ended up!

options.text: But that was exactly what they’d planned.
So there’s rarely anything left on the beach by visitors.
They hoped this would help them to find the person who’d filmed it.
But that isn’t all he expects to find.
It had begun its extraordinary journey across the sea.
They were a great success.
Unfortunately, he then forgot all about it, and left it there.
They immediately contacted the couple.

explanations.html:
16. G: [Cause and result]
Đáp án G đúng vì câu trước nói William đặt máy ảnh trên tảng đá để quay video, còn câu sau nói cả gia đình đi về mà máy ảnh vẫn còn ở đó. Câu Unfortunately, he then forgot all about it, and left it there giải thích chính xác vì sao điều đó xảy ra. Từ it ở đây quay lại chỉ the camera.

17. B: [Logical conclusion / Contrast]
Đáp án B đúng vì câu trước nói hòn đảo là khu bảo tồn chim và không ai được phép lên đảo nếu không có sự cho phép. Từ đó suy ra hiếm khi có đồ vật do khách để lại trên bãi biển. Câu sau mở đầu bằng However để tạo đối lập: dù vậy, những thứ biển đánh dạt vào vẫn thường xuyên được tìm thấy.

18. E: [Narrative development]
Đáp án E đúng vì câu trước mô tả những giây cuối của video chỉ còn nước xung quanh máy ảnh. Câu It had begun its extraordinary journey across the sea giải thích rằng chính lúc đó chiếc máy ảnh đã bắt đầu hành trình qua biển. Điều này nối rất hợp với thông tin trước đó rằng nó đã bị nước biển cuốn đi đến một hòn đảo gần Đức.

19. C: [Purpose]
Đáp án C đúng vì câu trước nói cặp đôi đăng một video lên mạng xã hội để xem có ai nhận ra không. Câu They hoped this would help them to find the person who’d filmed it diễn tả chính xác mục đích của hành động đó. Câu sau tiếp tục kể kết quả khi một chương trình máy tính xác định được địa điểm quay.

20. H: [Sequence]
Đáp án H đúng vì câu trước nói một người bạn của William đọc được tin trên báo và báo cho gia đình. Bước tiếp theo hợp lý nhất là gia đình liên lạc ngay với cặp đôi đã tìm thấy máy ảnh. Sau đó việc William và bố được mời tới hòn đảo trở nên hoàn toàn logic.

title: Julie Danes, teenage comedy performer
content_html:
Julie Danes is in many ways like any other 14-year-old. She enjoys having a laugh, and making other people laugh. The only difference is that when Julie tells a joke, she’s often in front of an audience of hundreds of people. She’s a successful comedian in Boston, in the USA.

Since Julie started as a comedian, her performance schedule has become busier and busier. [16] She thinks that she’s probably performed in public about 60 times at theatres all around the city.

Even though she’s much younger than most of the other comedians in Boston, she’s not in fact the only young person doing comedy on stage there. [17] However, it’s really only age that they have in common – Julie says their styles are very different. When she goes on stage, people in the audience are curious about the fact that she’s quite young for a comedian. [18] But Julie’s act also covers lots of other topics, just like those of older comedians.

When she first became interested in performing comedy, she felt slightly anxious about telling her parents of her plans. [19] Despite this, they still haven’t been to one of her shows. They don’t want her to feel under extra pressure – she might feel more nervous than usual if they were there.

[20] And when that day comes, Julie’s mum and dad will make sure they are in the audience. And they’ll certainly be very proud parents!

options.text: And so of course, they all enjoy her jokes about that.
Many of these theatres are great places to perform.
But when she did, they were pleased, and encouraged her to try it.
They’ve travelled all over the USA to watch her perform.
In fact, she now appears on stage about once a week.
Another 14-year-old, Brandon Phillips, frequently performs at the same shows.
Julie’s promised to let them know as soon as she feels ready for them to see her.
That’s why she likes appearing in front of so many people.

explanations.html:
16. E: [Detail / Addition]
Đáp án E đúng vì câu trước nói lịch biểu diễn của Julie ngày càng bận hơn, còn câu sau cho biết tổng số lần cô đã diễn công khai. Câu In fact, she now appears on stage about once a week bổ sung rất hợp về tần suất biểu diễn hiện tại và nối mượt với thông tin sau đó.

17. F: [Example / Reference]
Đáp án F đúng vì câu trước nói Julie không phải là người trẻ duy nhất diễn comedy ở Boston. Câu Another 14-year-old, Brandon Phillips, frequently performs at the same shows đưa ra ví dụ cụ thể. Câu sau dùng they để chỉ Julie và Brandon, nên liên kết rất chặt.

18. A: [Cause and effect]
Đáp án A đúng vì câu trước nói khán giả tò mò vì Julie còn khá trẻ đối với một comedian. Câu And so of course, they all enjoy her jokes about that cho thấy Julie biến chính sự tò mò đó thành chất liệu gây cười. Câu sau dùng also để nói rằng ngoài chủ đề tuổi tác, cô còn nói về nhiều chủ đề khác.

19. C: [Sequence / Contrast]
Đáp án C đúng vì câu trước nói Julie lo lắng về việc nói với bố mẹ, còn câu này cho biết khi cô thực sự nói ra, bố mẹ lại ủng hộ và khuyến khích cô thử. Câu sau mở đầu bằng Despite this, cho thấy dù bố mẹ phản ứng tích cực, họ vẫn chưa tới xem show nào của cô.

20. G: [Reference / Future plan]
Đáp án G đúng vì câu sau có cụm when that day comes, nên cần một câu trước đó xác định day là ngày nào. Câu Julie’s promised to let them know as soon as she feels ready for them to see her tạo đúng mốc đó: ngày Julie cảm thấy đã sẵn sàng để bố mẹ tới xem.

title: Lost Irish beach returns after 33 years
content_html:
Achill Island, off the coast of Ireland, is a popular tourist destination. It has beautiful mountains and clean beaches. The island was suddenly in the news in 1984 because of something that happened near the village of Dooagh. [16] They discovered that their beach had disappeared during the night, leaving behind just rocks. For years after that, only small amounts of sand returned to the once-beautiful shore, and fewer and fewer tourists went there. [17] The effect of this was felt by everyone in the small community.

Then, in 2017, something incredible happened. People couldn’t believe their eyes when, one morning, they found that their beach had returned, and the rocks were once again covered in soft golden sand! [18] They said that the amount of sand under the sea near the coast had slowly increased. Then, during a period of strong winds and powerful waves, most of it was carried to the shore.

The people of the island were of course delighted to have their beach back, and for some time, it attracted hundreds of visitors. [19] Business owners in the area were also hopeful that the beach would encourage surfers to return to the region, making it a popular holiday destination again. [20] No one was sure whether it was absolutely safe.

Later, a special type of seaweed – a plant that grows in the sea – appeared on the beach. It is traditionally used to help crops grow, so its return was particularly good news. The local people are hopeful that now the sand is finally here to stay.

options.text: They were able to experience these amazing events.
At first, however, people were advised to avoid going into the sea.
Losing so many visitors meant that many hotels and restaurants had to close.
But a most surprising thing happened that night.
Scientists from an Irish university provided a possible explanation.
Despite these benefits to plants and animals, a few people were disappointed.
One morning, the people living there had a nasty shock.
Tourists wanted to see the power of nature for themselves.

explanations.html:
16. G: [Context / Reference]
Đáp án G đúng vì câu sau bắt đầu bằng They discovered..., nên trước đó cần xuất hiện chủ thể rõ ràng là the people living there. Câu One morning, the people living there had a nasty shock giới thiệu đúng chủ thể này và tạo mạch rất tự nhiên dẫn vào việc họ phát hiện bãi biển đã biến mất.

17. C: [Cause and effect]
Đáp án C đúng vì câu trước nói fewer and fewer tourists went there, còn câu sau nói The effect of this was felt by everyone in the small community. Câu Losing so many visitors meant that many hotels and restaurants had to close giải thích chính xác ảnh hưởng của việc mất khách du lịch đối với cộng đồng địa phương.

18. E: [Reference / Explanation]
Đáp án E đúng vì câu sau mở đầu bằng They said..., nên trước chỗ trống cần có một nhóm người cụ thể để they quay lại chỉ. Câu Scientists from an Irish university provided a possible explanation đáp ứng đúng điều đó, đồng thời phù hợp với nội dung tiếp theo là phần giải thích khoa học về việc cát quay trở lại.

19. H: [Reason]
Đáp án H đúng vì câu trước nói bãi biển đã thu hút hàng trăm visitors, và câu này giải thích lý do của hiện tượng đó: Tourists wanted to see the power of nature for themselves. Câu sau tiếp tục nói về hy vọng khu vực này sẽ trở thành điểm du lịch nổi tiếng trở lại, nên mạch ý về du khách rất liền.

20. B: [Contrast / Safety]
Đáp án B đúng vì câu trước nói người dân và doanh nghiệp hy vọng bãi biển sẽ kéo du khách quay lại, nhưng câu sau lại nói No one was sure whether it was absolutely safe. Câu At first, however, people were advised to avoid going into the sea tạo sự chuyển ý rất hợp lý từ niềm hy vọng sang sự thận trọng về độ an toàn.
